data.description:
## What
E2 cluster: port the read-ish / quality category-B commands to in-process Python, one command (or tight
sibling pair) at a time, following the revised flow (port -> route argparse in-process -> rewire library
off _run -> golden parity test python==bash; NO bash deletion — that consolidates in E7/E8). Commands:
get-file-impact, get-verify-commands, exists, resolve, format, list-descendants, clarity-check, check-ac,
quality-check, summary, delete. Each reuses rebar.reducer / rebar.graph / rebar._engine_support
(resolver, output, reads) — never re-implemented. Byte-parity (stdout/stderr/exit, text + --output json)
captured from the bash dispatcher arm before routing, pinned by a test.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 11 E2 commands dispatch in-process via rebar._cli (added to an in-process router set)
- [ ] Each command's library function (where one exists) no longer subprocesses the dispatcher (_run)
- [ ] Per-command golden parity test pins python==bash (stdout/stderr/exit; text + json where applicable)
- [ ] Single-reducer show==list==search shape (f026) + exit-code + schema contracts preserved
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green after each command; isolated probe still 141/141
- [ ] No bash deleted in E2 (deletion is E7/E8); bash stays as the parity-pinned second impl
data.title: E2: port read-ish/quality category-B commands in-process (port+route+rewire+parity)
